reasoning: The crowd forecast is around 1.16%. The U.S. Congress is notoriously slow at passing comprehensive tech regulation (as seen with data privacy, which has taken years without federal action). The Republican control of the House/Senate/Presidency in the coming term likely reduces the probability of substantive, heavily regulated AI frameworks passing before 2026. The 119th Congress starts in January 2025, leaving only one year. With competing priorities, the chance of substantive AI model regulation being enacted into law in 2025 is extremely low. Therefore, I agree with the crowd's ~1.2% estimate.
